decisions.title: Commit to CSRS (Columnar Storage Read Service) as DLv3 foundation
decisions.context: Multiple CSRS integration tickets (ENG-9987, ENG-10160, ENG-9989, ENG-9493, ENG-10117) indicate we're building next-gen data layer. Shadow comparison issues suggest we're not yet at production parity. This is critical infrastructure for 'Nail the Core' - faster queries enable more decisions captured.
decisions.blockedBy: Shadow comparison false negatives (ENG-10117) and query forwarding logic incomplete (ENG-10160)

arguments.for: Enables 10x faster queries per roadmap; unlocks Canvas performance; required for Ask SIFT responsiveness; competitive moat
arguments.against: Production parity not achieved; dedup asymmetry creates data integrity risk; diverts resources from feature work

decisions.title: Prioritize Explore V2 table/chart polish over new chart types
decisions.context: ENG-8063 (bytes in charts), ENG-7662 (hex display), ENG-9884 (preset configs) are UX polish. ENG-10063/9537 (candidate runs) are workflow improvements. These are 'Embed Deeper' plays - making existing workflows delightful drives daily usage and decisions captured.

arguments.for: Low-hanging fruit for user delight; discipline-specific presets reduce time-to-first-decision; hex display requested by firmware teams; directly increases decisions per session
arguments.against: Doesn't expand TAM; may be premature optimization before Canvas GA; resources could go to Python Rules beta expansion

decisions.title: Ship Sift Edge stability fixes before new feature work
decisions.context: ENG-9436 (SQL lock contention causing exits) is a critical reliability issue. Sift Edge is our 'Build the Moat' embedded play - can't evangelize if it crashes. FedRamp workspace provisioning (ENG-10156) suggests enterprise push.

arguments.for: Crashes destroy enterprise trust; blocks FedRamp certification path; embedded reliability is competitive moat; every crash loses potential decisions captured
arguments.against: Only one customer reported; may be environmental; edge cases shouldn't block feature velocity

decisions.title: Resolve Canvas import/export bugs before expanding beta
decisions.context: ENG-10022 (CSV export broken), ENG-10006 (UDF dependencies empty) are data integrity issues. Canvas is our 'Nail the Core' collaboration layer - broken exports kill trust. Want to expand beta but can't with these bugs.

arguments.for: Export is table stakes for handoff workflows; UDF dependencies are critical for change impact analysis; both directly block decisions (can't share = no decision); beta expansion blocked
arguments.against: Workarounds exist (manual CSV, ask teammates); affects <5% of sessions; new features drive more signups than bug fixes

decisions.title: Define family member run limits policy before scaling
decisions.context: ENG-9568 (run count hard limit) suggests we're hitting scale issues. Test Results offline backing (ENG-6764) and improvements (ENG-10173) indicate testing workflow investment. Families are multi-user collaboration - core to 'Embed Deeper'.

arguments.for: Prevents runaway storage costs; forces users to archive/clean up old data; maintains query performance; protects NorthStar metric integrity
arguments.against: Artificial limits frustrate power users; competitive products have unlimited runs; may push users to local solutions; reduces decisions captured if they hit limits

keyResults.name: Daily Active Users in Explore V2

timeAudit.weekSummary: High coordination week - CSRS architecture sync, Canvas beta review, Edge incident postmortem consuming eng bandwidth. Critical path: unblock CSRS parity testing.
alerts: Canvas export bug (ENG-10022) blocking 3 beta customers from workflows
CSRS shadow mode discrepancies delaying production cutover by 2 weeks
Sift Edge crash (ENG-9436) escalated by enterprise prospect during eval
12 eng tickets lack clear owner/deadline - decision debt accumulating
